title: Documentation & Metadata Refresh (0.18.0)
description: Correct genuine command-reference and npm-metadata drift against the shipped 0.18.0 codebase: complete COMMANDS.md (which claims to be a complete reference but omits five registered command groups), add the missing fleet + five commands to the README CLI quick-reference, and refresh package.json description/keywords to advertise the 0.18.0 surface. Docs and metadata only — no source, test, or git-tag changes.

clarificationQuestions.question: Should VISION.md and docs/providers.md be part of this refresh?

options.label: B
options.description: Exclude them — spot-check found them current

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: B — VISION.md:304,358 already documents the 0.16.0 lens-panel config and docs/providers.md:24,48,73 already covers deepseek/grok/claude-code/opus.

clarificationQuestions.question: Should CHANGELOG.md be edited as part of this refresh?

options.label: A
options.description: Add a CHANGELOG entry for the doc drift

options.label: B
options.description: No CHANGELOG change — the drift is reference-sync, not undocumented shipped work

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: B — CHANGELOG already runs through [0.18.0] 2026-06-23 with an [Unreleased] section; the affected commands already shipped and were changelogged.

clarificationQuestions.question: Are config/telemetry/worktree/memory/facts real user-facing commands that warrant documentation?
clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — all five are registered in src/cli/index.ts:312-324 with .description() text and none use { hidden: true } (grep found none).

clarificationQuestions.question: Should the README CLI block carry full per-subcommand detail for the five commands, or one-line entries deferring to COMMANDS.md?
clarificationQuestions.recommendation: B — mirrors the README's existing 'Full reference in COMMANDS.md' pattern (README.md:548), keeping README a quick-reference.

clarificationQuestions.question: Which package.json keywords are defensible additions for 0.18.0?

options.label: B
options.description: A broader marketing set including medical/telegram/calendar

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — each maps to a first-class documented CLI surface (security-audit command + docs/security-audit.md; fleet + docs/fleet.md; incident command; knowledge-platform docs).

resolvedClarifications.answer: Exclude VISION.md and docs/providers.md — both are current. VISION.md:304,358,366 documents the 0.16.0 lens-panel config; docs/providers.md:24,48,73,171 already covers deepseek/grok/claude-code/opus routing and cost.

No CHANGELOG change. CHANGELOG.md runs through [0.18.0] 2026-06-23 with an [Unreleased] section; the affected commands already shipped and were changelogged. The drift is a reference-sync gap, not undocumented shipped work.
Yes, document all five. config/telemetry/worktree/memory/facts are registered in src/cli/index.ts:312-324 with .description() text; grep for 'hidden' in each command file returns nothing.
One-line README entries deferring to COMMANDS.md, matching the existing pattern at README.md:548 ('Full reference in COMMANDS.md').

Add keywords security-audit, fleet, incident-response, knowledge-platform. Each maps to a shipped, documented CLI surface (src/cli/commands/security-audit.ts + docs/security-audit.md; src/fleet/index.ts + docs/fleet.md; src/cli/commands/incident.ts; docs/knowledge-platform.md).
Autonomous self-approval. Every assumption is backed by cited file evidence; no high-stakes or codebase-silent question remains, so proceed at status draft without user clarification.

approvalEvidence: COMMANDS.md:2 (claims 'Complete reference for all bober CLI commands')
README.md:507-633 (CLI quick-reference block omits fleet + the five commands)

assumptions: COMMANDS.md:2 declares itself the 'Complete reference for all bober CLI commands', so any registered non-hidden top-level command missing a heading is genuine drift.

config, telemetry, worktree, memory, facts are real user-facing commands: registered in src/cli/index.ts:312-324 with .description() text; grep 'hidden' in src/cli/commands/{config,telemetry,worktree,memory,facts}.ts returns nothing.

The fleet command family is a first-class feature (src/fleet/index.ts:477,525,571; docs/fleet.md; COMMANDS.md 'Fleet Commands' section) but absent from the README CLI quick-reference (README.md:507-633).
VISION.md and docs/providers.md are current (VISION.md:304,358 lens-panel config; docs/providers.md:24,48,73 deepseek/grok/claude-code/opus) and are excluded from this refresh.
Git tags v0.12.0-v0.18.0 all already exist and CHANGELOG.md runs through [0.18.0] 2026-06-23, so historical tagging and CHANGELOG re-cutting are out of scope.

package.json keyword additions are limited to shipped documented surfaces: security-audit (src/cli/commands/security-audit.ts + docs/security-audit.md), fleet (src/fleet/index.ts + docs/fleet.md), incident-response (src/cli/commands/incident.ts), knowledge-platform (docs/knowledge-platform.md).
outOfScope: Creating, moving, or pushing any git tags (v0.12.0-v0.18.0 all already exist).
Re-cutting or restructuring CHANGELOG.md released version blocks.
Any change to files under src/ or any *.test.ts (no application or test behavior change).
Changing package.json version, name, bin, dependencies, peerDependencies, or scripts.
Adding new CLI commands, MCP tools, or altering command registrations.
Broad marketing rewrites of README/COMMANDS beyond correcting the identified drift.

features.title: Complete the COMMANDS.md CLI reference
features.description: Add COMMANDS.md sections for the five registered but undocumented command groups (config, telemetry, worktree, memory, facts) so the file's 'complete reference' claim holds against the real CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: COMMANDS.md contains a '### `bober <cmd>`' heading for each of config, telemetry, worktree, memory, and facts.

AC2: Each new section documents that command's real subcommands (config migrate; telemetry status/purge/export; worktree run; memory distill/list/show/prune; facts add/list/show/invalidate) with no invented flags.
AC3: A completeness check over every top-level command registered in src/cli/index.ts and src/fleet/index.ts finds a matching COMMANDS.md heading (zero missing).

features.title: Sync the README CLI quick-reference
features.description: Add the fleet command family and one-line entries for config/telemetry/worktree/memory/facts to the README CLI block, each deferring to COMMANDS.md for full detail.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: The README CLI reference lists agent-bober fleet <manifest>, fleet expand, and fleet expand-deep.
AC2: The README CLI reference lists agent-bober usage lines for config, telemetry, worktree, memory, and facts, each pointing to COMMANDS.md.

features.title: Refresh package.json npm metadata for 0.18.0
features.description: Update the package.json description and keywords to advertise the 0.18.0 surface (security auditing, fleet orchestration, knowledge platform) without touching version, name, bin, deps, or scripts.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: package.json keywords includes security-audit, fleet, incident-response, and knowledge-platform.
AC2: package.json description mentions security auditing and fleet orchestration; version stays 0.18.0 and name/bin/dependencies/scripts are unchanged.

techNotes.dataModel: No data model — documentation Markdown (README.md, COMMANDS.md) and npm package metadata (package.json) mirroring the CLI command registrations in src/cli/index.ts and src/fleet/index.ts.
securityConsiderations: Docs-only change; no code paths, no new dependencies, no egress. No secrets or PHI are documented.
package.json edits limited to description/keywords — dependencies, bin, and scripts untouched, so no supply-chain surface change.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: COMMANDS.md remains a complete CLI reference: every registered non-hidden top-level command has a documentation heading.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Iterate the top-level commands from src/cli/index.ts + src/fleet/index.ts and assert each has a matching '### ' heading in COMMANDS.md.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The refresh introduces no code or test behavior change; the project still builds.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: npm run build exits 0 and git diff --name-only lists only COMMANDS.md, README.md, and package.json.

constraints: Docs and metadata only — no changes under src/ or any *.test.ts.
No git tag creation, moving, or pushing.
Preserve valid JSON in package.json and preserve version 0.18.0.
